programme.title: Live Premier League
programme.desc: Brentford v Fulham (Kick-off 12.30pm). Coverage of the top-flight match from Gtech Community Stadium, as the West London rivals go head to head. The visitors came from a goal down in the reverse fixture to win the game 3-1 at Craven Cottage. Mikkel Damsgaard had given the Bees the lead in the 20th minute but three goals inside 12 minutes from Alex Iwobi, Harry Wilson and an Ethan Pinnock own-goal turned the game on its head and gave Fulham the three points.

programme.title: Live: The Gallagher PREM
programme.desc: Exeter Chiefs v Northampton Saints (Kick-off 3.05pm). Coverage of the match from Sandy Park. The hosts produced an incredible second-half comeback in the reverse fixture in September, as they came from 33-7 down to earn themselves a 33-33 draw. The Saints scored five tries in the first half before the Chiefs fought back with four unanswered tries in the second period to take a share of the spoils.

programme.title: Live: Serie A
programme.desc: Napoli v Lazio. Coverage of the Italian top-flight match from Stadio Diego Armando Maradona, joining the action 15 minutes after kick-off.

programme.title: Live Premier League
programme.desc: Chelsea v Manchester United (Kick-off 8.00pm). Coverage of the top-flight match from Stamford Bridge. The visitors won the reverse fixture back in September in a game that was influenced by a fifth-minute red card for Chelsea keeper Robert Sanchez. The Spaniard took out Bryan Mbuemo as he was through on goal to leave the Blues with 10 men for the majority of the game as Bruno Fernandes and Casemeiro scored in the first half to give the Red Devils a two-goal lead. Casemeiro was sent off himself in first-half stoppage time but the visitors held on to win 2-1 despite Trevoh Chalobah's 80th-minute header.
